steps.title: Identify the subject number and time context
steps.content: The subject “Farouk El-Baz” is singular — the appositive phrase “a geologist and space scientist” provides description but doesn't create a compound subject. The phrase “during the 1960s and 1970s” establishes that past tense is required.

steps.title: Match the singular subject to its past-tense verb
steps.content: A singular subject requires a singular verb form; in past tense, the singular form of “to be” is “was”. The other choices fail: “are” and “were” are plural, and “have been” is present-tense. That matches choice B — B is correct.
